GOODYEAR SITE PLAN NOTES
1.
IMPROVEMENTS MADE WITHIN A CITY RIGHT-OF-WAY, TRACT, OR
EASEMENT THAT WILL BE MAINTAINED IN THE FUTURE BY THE CITY OF
GOODYEAR SHALL BE CLEARLY IDENTIFIES ON THE APPROVED PLANS
AND ARE SUBJECT TO THE FOLLOWING REQUIREMENTS.
IMPROVEMENTS MAY INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO STREET
SURFACES, CURB, GUTTER, SIDEWALKS, RAMPS, DRIVEWAYS, TURN
BAYS, BUS BAYS, STREET LIGHTING, SIGNAGE, AND STRIPING.
LANDSCAPE RESPONSIBILITIES ARE IDENTIFIED UNDER SEPARATE
NOTE:
a.
THE DEVELOPER SHALL MAINTAIN AND WARRANTY ALL 
IMPROVEMENTS FOR A PERIOD OF TWO YEARS BEGINNING
IMMEDIATELY AFTER THE CITY ISSUES THE NOTIFICATION OF
APPROVAL FOR THE PROJECT.
b.
DURING THE MAINTENANCE AND WARRANTY PERIOD, THE
DEVELOPER SHALL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR MAINTAINING
ADEQUATE PROTECTION OF ALL IMPROVEMENTS.  ANY DAMAGED
IMPROVEMENTS SHALL BE IMMEDIATELY REPAIRED OR
REPLACED AT THE DEVELOPER'S EXPENSE.
c.
MAINTENANCE SHALL INCLUDE BUT IS NOT LIMITED TO STREET
SWEEPING, APPLICATION OF SEALANT IN ALL PAVEMENT CRACKS
AND JOINTS, AND APPLICATION OF SEALANT OVER ALL ASPHALT
PAVEMENTS.
d.
AT THE TERMINATION OF THE MAINTENANCE AND WARRANTY
PERIOD, ALL IMPROVEMENTS SHALL BE UNDAMAGED AND SHALL
MEET CITY STANDARDS.
e.
IF ALL IMPROVEMENTS ARE NOT ACCEPTABLE AT THE END OF
THE TWO-YEAR PERIOD, THE MAINTENANCE AND WARRANTY
PERIOD SHALL CONTINUE UNTIL THE IMPROVEMENTS MEET CITY
APPROVAL.
2.
LANDSCAPED AREAS THAT WILL BE MAINTAINED IN THE FUTURE BY
THE CITY OF GOODYEAR SHALL BE CLEARLY IDENTIFIED ON THE
APPROVED PLANS AND ARE SUBJECT TO THE FOLLOWING 
REQUIREMENTS.  THESE AREAS MAY INCLUDE RETENTION BASINS,
PARKS, RIGHTS-OF-WAY, AND STREET CENTER MEDIAN LANDSCAPING.
a.
THE DEVELOPER SHALL MAINTAIN AND WARRANTY ALL 
LANDSCAPE IMPROVEMENTS, INCLUDING PLANTED AREAS AND
IRRIGATION SYSTEMS FOR A PERIOD OF TWO YEARS BEGINNING
IMMEDIATELY AFTER THE CITY ISSUES THE NOTIFICATION OF
APPROVAL FOR THE PROJECT.
b.
DURING THE MAINTENANCE AND WARRANTY PERIOD, THE
DEVELOPER SHALL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR MAINTAINING
ADEQUATE PROTECTION OF ALL AREAS.  ANY DAMAGED
PLANTING AND/OR IRRIGATION SYSTEMS SHALL BE IMMEDIATELY
REPAIRED OR REPLACED AT THE DEVELOPER'S EXPENSE.
c.
MAINTENANCE SHALL INCLUDE CONTINUOUS OPERATIONS OF
WATERING, WEEDING, REMOVAL OF DEAD PLANT MATERIAL,
MOWING, ROLLING, FERTILIZING, SPRAYING, REPLACEMENT, AND
ALL OTHER MEASURES NECESSARY TO ENSURE NORMAL
HEALTHY GROWTH.
d.
WHEN THE TURF HAS ESTABLISHED SUFFICIENT ROOT
STRUCTURE AND HAS OBTAINED AN APPROXIMATE HEIGHT OF 3
INCHES, MOWING SHALL BEGIN IMMEDIATELY TO ARCHIVE A
TURF HEIGHT OF 2 INCHES;  THE TURF SHALL BE MOWED
THEREAFTER TO SAFELY MAINTAIN THE 2 INCH HEIGHT.
e.
AT THE TERMINATION OF THE MAINTENANCE AND WARRANTY
PERIOD, ALL TURF AREAS SHALL BE LIVE, HEALTHY,
UNDAMAGED, AND FREE OF INFESTATIONS.  ALL AREAS SHALL
BE COMPLETELY VOID OF BARREN SPOTS LARGER THAN 3
INCHES BY 3 INCHES.
f.
IF ALL PLANTINGS ARE NOT ACCEPTABLE AT THE END OF THE
TWO-YEAR PERIOD, THE MAINTENANCE AND WARRANTY PERIOD
SHALL CONTINUE UNTIL THE LANDSCAPING MEETS CITY
APPROVAL.

CITY OF PHOENIX NOTES
• 
DEVELOPMENT AND USE OF THIS SITE WILL CONFORM WITH ALL APPLICABLE 
CODES AND ORDINANCES.  
• 
THIS PROJECT IS LOCATED IN THE CITY OF PHOENIX WATER SERVICE AREA 
AND HAS BEEN DESIGNATED AS HAVING AN ASSURED WATER SUPPLY. 
• 
ALL NEW OR RELOCATED UTILITIES WILL BE PLACED UNDERGROUND.  
• 
ALL SIGNAGE REQUIRES SEPARATE APPROVALS AND PERMITS. 
• 
ANY LIGHTING WILL BE PLACED SO AS TO DIRECT LIGHT AWAY FROM THE 
ADJACENT RESIDENTIAL DISTRICTS AND WILL NOT EXCEED ONE-FOOT CANDLE 
AT THE PROPERTY LINE.  NO NOISE, ODOR, OR VIBRATION WILL BE EMITTED 
SO THAT IT EXCEEDS THE GENERAL LEVEL OF NOISE, ODOR, OR VIBRATION 
EMITTED BY USES OUTSIDE OF THE SITE. 
• 
OWNERS OF PROPERTY ADJACENT TO PUBLIC RIGHT-OF-WAY WILL HAVE THE
RESPONSIBILITY FOR MAINTAINING ALL LANDSCAPING WITHIN THE RIGHTS-OF-
WAY IN ACCORDANCE WITH APPROVED PLANS. 
LEGAL DESCRIPTION
A PORTION OF THE WEST HALF OF SECTION 12,TOWNSHIP 4 NORTH, RANGE 2 EAST 
OF THE GILA AND SALT RIVER MERIDIAN, MARICOPA COUNTY, ARIZONA , AND 
DESCRIBED AS
FOLLOWS:
COMMENCING AT THE BRASS CAP IN HAND HOLE FOUND AT THE NORTH QUARTER 
CORNER OF SAID SECTION 12, FROM WHICH THE BRASS CAP IN HAND HOLE FOUND 
AT THE NORTHWEST CORNER OF SAID SECTION 12 BEARS N.89°39'15"W., A 
DISTANCE OF 2674.69 FEET;
THENCE S.00°09'22"E. ALONG THE EAST LINE OF SAID WEST HALF OF SECTION 12, A
DISTANCE OF 2689.21 FEET;
THENCE DEPARTING SAID EAST LINE, S.89°47'32"W., A DISTANCE OF 40.00 FEET TO 
A POINT ON THE EXISTING WEST RIGHT OF WAY LINE FOR 23RD AVENUE AND THE 
POINT OF BEGINNING;
THENCE S.00°09'22"E., ALONG SAID EXISTING WEST RIGHT OF WAY LINE, A 
DISTANCE OF 192.92 FEET TO A POINT ON THE PROPOSED WEST RIGHT OF WAY 
LINE FOR 23RD AVENUE;
THENCE DEPARTING SAID EXISTING WEST RIGHT OF WAY LINE FOR 23RD AVENUE,
S.00°59'23"W., ALONG SAID PROPOSED WEST RIGHT OF WAY LINE FOR 23RD 
AVENUE, A DISTANCE OF 573.42 FEET;
THENCE DEPARTING SAID PROPOSED WEST RIGHT OF WAY LINE FOR 23RD 
AVENUE, S.45°25'00"W., A DISTANCE OF 50.68 FEET TO A POINT ON THE PROPOSED 
NORTHERLY RIGHT OF WAY LINE FOR QUESTA DRIVE;
THENCE S.89°50'14"W., ALONG SAID PROPOSED NORTHERLY RIGHT OF WAY LINE, A
DISTANCE OF 339.24 FEET TO THE BEGINNING OF A CURVE CONCAVE 
NORTHEASTERLY AND HAVING A RADIUS OF 755.00 FEET;
THENCE NORTHWESTERLY ALONG SAID PROPOSED CURVED NORTHERLY RIGHT OF 
WAY LINE, THROUGH A CENTRAL ANGLE OF 39°38'19", AN ARC LENGTH OF 522.33 
FEET;
THENCE DEPARTING NON-TANGENT TO SAID PROPOSED CURVED NORTHERLY 
RIGHT OF WAY LINE FOR QUESTA DRIVE, N. 42°43'14"E., A DISTANCE OF 59.62 FEET 
TO THE BEGINNING OF A CURVE CONCAVE SOUTHEASTERLY AND HAVING A RADIUS 
OF 457.50 FEET;
THENCE NORTHEASTERLY ALONG SAID CURVE THROUGH A CENTRAL ANGLE OF 15°
35'21", AN ARC LENGTH OF 124.48 FEET;
THENCE N.58°18'35"E., A DISTANCE OF 5450.42 FEET TO THE BEGINNING OF A 
CURVE CONCAVE NORTHWESTERLY AND HAVING A RADIUS OF 542.50 FEET;
THENCE NORTHEASTERLY ALONG SAID CURVE THROUGH A CENTRAL ANGLE OF 32°
18'02", AN ARC LENGTH OF 305.84 FEET;
THENCE DEPARTING NON-TANGEGNT TO SAID CURVE, N.89°47'32"E., A DISTANCE OF 
68.22 FEET TO THE POINT OF BEGINNING.
THE ABOVE DESCRIBED NET PARCEL CONTAINS 394,072 SQUARE FEET OR 9.05 
ACRES MORE OR LESS

A. ALL DEVELOPMENT AND USE OF THE SITE SHALL CONFORM TO ALL 
APPLICABLE CODES AND ORDINANCES.
B. SEE CIVIL AND LANDSCAPE DOCUMENTS FOR ADDITIONAL COORDINATION 
ITEMS.
C. SEE ELECTRICAL DOCUMENTS FOR SITE LIGHTING AND SITE RELATED ITEMS 
REQUIRING SERVICE.
D. SEE ENLARGED PLANS FOR ADDITIONAL HARDSCAPE, DRIVE, AND PARKING 
DATA.
E. SEE SOILS REPORT FOR SITE AND FOOTING SOIL PREPARATION GUIDELINES, 
AND ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS FOR SLOPES AT BUILDING PERIMETERS.
F. SEE SOILS REPORT AND CIVIL DOCUMENTS FOR PAVING SECTIONS AND 
CONCRETE DESIGN FOR ALL HARDSCAPE.
G. THE GENERAL CONTRACTOR IS TO VISIT THE SITE FOR OBSERVATION OF 
ANY EXISTING CONDITIONS AND SHALL REPORT ANY AND ALL CONFLICTS 
BETWEEN THE DOCUMENTS AND THE FIELD CONDITIONS.
H. ALL EXISTING SITE EQUIPMENT AND FEATURES, I.E. LANDSCAPING, LIGHTING, 
SIDEWALKS, TRANSFORMERS, TEL. CO. BOXES, WATER METERS ETC. SHALL 
BE PROTECTED FROM DAMAGE UNLESS OTHERWISE NOTED TO BE REMOVED 
OR RELOCATED.
I. 
ALL AREAS WITH PEDESTRIAN ACCESS SHALL COMPLY WITH THE AMERICANS 
WITH DISABILITIES ACT AND LOCAL ENFORCED CODES. THERE WILL BE NO 
EXCEPTIONS.
J. ALL CURBS AT ACCESSIBLE PATHS / PARKING ARE TO BE FLUSH TO 
PAVEMENT TO PROVIDE REQUIRED RAMP ACCESS.
K. ALL ACCESSIBLE ROUTES:
a. SHALL HAVE AN 1/2" OR LESS CHANGE IN ELEVATION AT EXTERIOR 
"ACCESSIBLE" DOORS;
b. SHALL HAVE ROUGH, NON-SLIP SURFACE FINISH AT ELEVATION CHANGES.
L. ALL SITE EQUIPMENT SHALL BE PAINTED TO MATCH ADJACENT BUILDING, SITE 
WALL, OR LANDSCAPE, AS APPLIES AND IN THAT ORDER OF PREFERENCE.
M. OFFSITE LIGHTING SHALL BE COORDINATED WITH LOCAL UTILITY COMPANY.
N. FIRE LANES SHALL BE PROVIDED IN COMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL A.H.J
O. ALL SIGNS REQUIRE SEPARATE PERMITS AND APPROVALS.
P. SEE PROJECT MANUAL FOR ADDITIONAL ITEMS OF COORDINATION AND 
SPECIFIC  FOR PRODUCTS AND PROCEDURES.
